title: malva tournefortiana var. lusitanica
intro: Malva tournefortiana var. lusitanica, also known as the Lusitanian mallow, is a variety of the Malva tournefortiana plant, a flowering plant belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The Lusitanian mallow has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for soothing skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The plant is also rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals such as potassium and magnesium, providing health benefits like reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the Lusitanian mallow can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for a range of uses in traditional medicine and natural remedies.
